title: Prestwick Airport: John Swinney seeking UK Government meeting over US military use of airport

sections.heading: Why Swinney wants answers
sections.content: First Minister John Swinney is seeking a meeting with the UK Government after American military aircraft used Prestwick Airport on journeys linked to the Middle East. The Scotsman reported that flight logs showed aircraft involved in supporting US military action had passed through the Scottish Government-owned airport in recent weeks.

sections.heading: Aircraft movements cited
sections.content: The report said three Boeing KC-135 Stratotankers stopped at Prestwick in February after leaving Riverside Air Force Base in California. Between 19 and 22 February they flew on to Saudi Arabia before deploying in Israel, and one of the aircraft was later tracked flying east towards Iran on 28 February. Since the start of March, all three appeared to be based at Ben Gurion Airport near Tel Aviv.

sections.heading: Political and official responses
sections.content: Patrick Harvie of the Scottish Greens said ministers now had to explain what they knew about the flights and why US Air Force aircraft were not being subjected to the same scrutiny previously applied to Israeli military aircraft. The Scottish Government said it does not receive information on the purpose of military flights using Scottish airports and had asked the UK Government for clarity, while the Ministry of Defence said Prestwick was not being used to launch US military strikes.
